atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -32,6 +32,14 @@ pub trait CanonStateSubscriptions: NodePrimitivesProvider + Send + Sync {
atoms.semantic_claim: CanonStateSubscriptions exposes a blocking canonical-state subscription API whose handlers are part of the canonical-state notification contract and must run synchronously before async canonical-state broadcasts.

destinations.evidence: The destination trait contains the same blocking canonical-state subscription method and the same handler type.

The destination implementation registers blocking handlers and invokes them before the async broadcast; the regression test asserts the async receiver cannot observe the notification before the blocking handler runs.
The handler type and synchronous notifier implementation required by the trait contract are present in the port.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Register a blocking handler that attempts to read from the async canonical-state receiver during notify_canon_state; the test should fail if the async broadcast is observable before the blocking handler completes.
atoms.notes: Exact trait/API mapping is supported by direct implementation and regression evidence, so no adversarial review is required.

atoms.semantic_claim: The blanket CanonStateSubscriptions implementation for references forwards the new blocking subscription method to the underlying provider, preserving the API for borrowed providers.

destinations.evidence: The destination reference blanket implementation contains the same forwarding method.

atoms.notes: Reference forwarding is an exact API compatibility mapping.

summary: Blocking canonical-state subscription API additions in notifications.rs are exactly present in the v2.2.0 port.
notes: The remaining stream punctuation changes in the patch are rustfmt-only noise and were not counted as behavior atoms.
